fields.INSTRUCTION_CONTENT:
{"resting": "Ask the patient to be in supine posture and should breath normally and should not make any body movements to avoid erroneous results.

Check ECG cable connections and ECG Electrodes placement."}

{"breathing": "Ask the patient to be in supine posture and required to inhale slowly till Green light OFF and Exhale slowly till Red light OFF alternatively 6 times.

Check ECG cable connections and ECG Electrodes placement."}

{"supine": "Ask the patient to be in supine posture and should breath normally.

Check ECG cable connections and ECG Electrodes placement.", "standing": "Ask the patient to stand immediately.

Check ECG cable connections and ECG Electrodes placement."}

{"valsalva": "Ask the patient to be in sitting posture. When Green light ON ask the patient to blow with mouth piece at 40mmHg constant pressure upto Red light ON.

Check ECG cable connections and ECG Electrodes placement."}

{"supine": "Ask the patient to be in supine posture and blood pressure Cuff should be connected to the dominant arm.

Check BP Cuff and air tubing connection properly to avoid leakage and error.", "standing": "Ask the patient to stand immediately and click start to take measurement.

Check BP Cuff and air tubing connection properly to avoid leakage and error.", "interval": "Ask the patient to wait for 3 minutes in standing posture. After 1 minute BP measurement starts automatically.

Check BP Cuff and air tubing connection properly to avoid leakage and error."}

{"before_handgrip": "Ask the patient to be in sitting posture and blood pressure Cuff should be connected to the dominant arm.

Check BP Cuff and air tubing connection properly to avoid leakage and error.", "after_handgrip": "Ask the patient to be in sitting posture and hold the bar in non dominant arm as tightly as possible. Blood pressure Cuff should be connected to the dominant arm as tightly as possible. Check BP Cuff and air tubing connection properly to avoid leakage and error.
Click start to take measurement."}

{"hrv": "Ask the patient to be in supine posture and should breath normally and should not make any body movements for 5 minutes to avoid erroneous results.

Check ECG cable connections and ECG Electrodes placement."}

{"resting": "
  1. Switch ON Hardware Unit
  2. Check USB or Bluetooth connection between KODYSCAN Hardware unit and computer.
  3. Ask the patient to be in supine position.
  4. Connect the four ECG Leads as shown in the connection Diagram.
  5. Let the patient relax for 1 minute.
  6. Explain the patient that He/she has to breath normally and should not move.
  7. Now the test starts. Follow the instructions on the test screen.
  8. Wait for the proper & stable ECG signal then click ‘Record’ button to record the test.
  9. Wait until the test ends automatically and results are displayed.
  10. In case of emergency you may cancel the test by clicking the Cancel button on the screen.
"}

{"breathing": "
  1. Switch ON Hardware Unit.
  2. Check USB or Bluetooth connection between KODYSCAN Hardware unit and computer.
  3. Ask the patient to be in supine position.
  4. Connect the four ECG Leads as shown in the connection Diagram.
  5. Let the patient relax for 1 minute.
  6. Explain the patient that He/she must very slowly INHALE when the GREEN LED glows and very slowly EXHALE when the RED LED glows in the External Panel.
  7. Now the test starts. Follow the instructions on the test screen.
  8. After a proper patient respiratory rhythm synchronised with External Panel LED’s is established click on ‘Record’ button to record the test.
  9. Wait until the test ends automatically and results are displayed.
  10. In case of emergency you may cancel the test by clicking the Cancel button on the screen.
"}

{"standing": "
  1. Switch ON Hardware Unit.
  2. Check USB or Bluetooth connection between KODYSCAN Hardware unit and computer.
  3. Ask the patient to be in supine position.
  4. Connect the four ECG Leads as shown in the connection Diagram.
  5. Let the patient relax for 1 minute.
  6. Explain the patient that He/she has to Stand UP when the RED LED glows on the External Panel box.
  7. Now the test starts. Follow the instructions on the test screen.
  8. Wait for the proper & stable ECG signal then click ‘Record’ button to record the test After the RED LED glows, patient stands up on his own. IMMEDIATELY click on ‘Record’ button to record the test.
  9. Wait until the test ends automatically and results are displayed.
  10. In case of emergency you may cancel the test by clicking the Cancel button on the screen.
"}

{"valsalva": "
  1. Switch ON Hardware Unit.
  2. Check USB or Bluetooth connection between KODYSCAN Hardware unit and computer.
  3. Ask the patient to be in supine position.
  4. Connect the four ECG leads as shown in the connection Diagram.
  5. Let the patient relax for 1 minute.
  6. Explain the patient about Valsalva maneuver When GREEN LED glows He/she has to blow into the mouthpiece up to 40mmHg until RED LED glows in the External Panel box.
  7. Now the test starts. Follow the instruction on the test screen.
  8. Wait for the proper & stable ECG signal then click ‘Record’ button to record the test After the GREEN LED glows, assist the patient to blow upto 40mmHg pressure and the patient has to maintain the pressure and stop blowing when RED LED glows.
  9. Wait until the test ends automatically and results are displayed.
  10. In case of emergency you may cancel the test by clicking the Cancel button on the screen.
"}

{"postural": "
  1. Switch ON Hardware Unit.
  2. Check USB or Bluetooth connection between KODYSCAN Hardware unit and computer.
  3. Ask the patient to be in supine position.
  4. Connect the BP cuff to patient’s dominant arm as shown in the connection Diagram.
  5. Let the patient relax for 1 minute.
  6. Explain the patient that He/she has to Stand UP when the RED LED glows on the External Panel box.
  7. Now the test starts. Follow the instruction on the test screen.
  8. After a few seconds click on ‘Start BP’ button to record the test. After the RED LED glows patient stands up on his own. IMMEDIATELY click on After standing test. Wait for the results to be displayed.
  9. After 1-minute BP measurement starts automatically.
  10. Wait until the test ends automatically and results are displayed
  11. In case of emergency you may cancel the test by clicking the Cancel button on the screen.
"}

{"sustained": "
  1. Switch ON Hardware Unit.
  2. Check USB or Bluetooth connection between KODYSCAN Hardware unit and computer.
  3. Ask the patient to be in supine position.
  4. Connect the BP cuff to patient’s dominant arm as shown in the connection Diagram.
  5. Let the patient relax for 1 minute.
  6. Explain the patient that He/she has to GRIP the bar with his non dominant hand as tight as possible when RED LED glows on the External Panel box.
  7. Now the test starts. Follow the instruction on the test screen.
  8. After a few seconds, click on ‘Start BP’ button to record the test. After the RED LED glows ask the patient to GRIP the bar as tight as possible. Assist the patient in sustaining the grip as long as possible without releasing the pressure..
  9. Wait until the test ends automatically and results are displayed
  10. In case of emergency you may cancel the test by clicking the Cancel button on the screen.
"}

{"hrv": "
  1. Switch ON Hardware Unit
  2. Check USB or Bluetooth connection between KODYSCAN Hardware unit and computer.
  3. Ask the patient to be in supine position.
  4. Connect the four ECG Leads as shown in the connection Diagram.
  5. Let the patient relax for 1 minute.
  6. Explain the patient that He/she has to breath normally and should not move.
  7. Now the test starts. Follow the instructions on the test screen.
  8. Wait for the proper & stable ECG signal then click ‘Record’ button to record the test.
  9. Wait until the test ends automatically and results are displayed.
  10. In case of emergency you may cancel the test by clicking the Cancel button on the screen.
"}
